title: calceolaria vaccinioides
intro: Calceolaria vaccinioides, also known as the 'Pigeon-milk flower', is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. It is native to South America, particularly in the Andes mountains. Calceolaria vaccinioides is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant and demulcent, which can help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, providing potential health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues and promoting digestive comfort.
